# Contributing Guide

Thank you for your interest in contributing! We welcome contributions of all kinds, including bug reports, feature requests, documentation improvements, and code changes.

## Table of Contents

- [Code of Conduct](#code-of-conduct)
- [Getting Started](#getting-started)
- [Development Setup](#development-setup)
- [Branching](#branching)
- [Making Changes](#making-changes)
- [Testing](#testing)
- [Commit Messages](#commit-messages)
- [Pull Requests](#pull-requests)
- [Reporting Issues](#reporting-issues)
- [Style Guidelines](#style-guidelines)
- [License](#license)

---

## Code of Conduct

By participating in this project, you agree to interact respectfully and constructively. Be professional, inclusive, and considerate of others.

---

## Getting Started

1. Fork the repository.
2. Clone your fork:

   ```bash
   git clone https://github.com/ShakAsante/ui-x.git
   ```

3. Navigate into the project:

4. Create a new branch:

   ```bash
   git checkout -b feature/your-feature-name
   ```

---

## Development Setup

Install project dependencies:

```bash
wally install
```

Run the development environment according to the project's documentation.

---

## Branching

Use descriptive branch names:

- `feature/add-login`
- `fix/api-timeout`
- `docs/update-readme`
- `refactor/auth-module`

---

## Making Changes

- Keep changes focused on a single issue or feature.
- Write clean, maintainable code.
- Update documentation when applicable.
- Add or update tests for new functionality.
- Avoid unrelated formatting-only changes.

---

## Testing

Before submitting a pull request:

1. Run all tests.
2. Ensure linting passes.
3. Verify that the project builds successfully.

---

## Commit Messages

Follow clear, descriptive commit messages.

```
feat: add user profile endpoint
fix: resolve null pointer exception
docs: update installation guide
test: add authentication tests
refactor: simplify validation logic
```

---

## Pull Requests

Before opening a pull request:

- [ ] Code builds successfully.
- [ ] Tests pass.
- [ ] Documentation has been updated if needed.
- [ ] No unnecessary files are included.
- [ ] Commit history is clean.

Pull requests should include:

- A summary of changes
- Motivation for the change
- Screenshots (if UI changes)
- Related issue references (e.g. `Closes #42`)

---

## Reporting Issues

When creating an issue, please include:

- Description of the problem
- Steps to reproduce
- Expected behavior
- Actual behavior
- Environment information
  - Operating System
  - Language/runtime version
  - Project version
- Relevant logs or screenshots

---

## Style Guidelines

- Follow the project's coding conventions.
- Keep functions small and focused.
- Prefer readable code over clever code.
- Document public APIs.
- Remove unused code before submitting.

---

## Documentation

Documentation improvements are always welcome.

Examples include:

- Fixing typos
- Improving examples
- Clarifying instructions
- Adding tutorials
- Updating outdated information

---

## Questions

If you're unsure about a change, open an issue or start a discussion before investing significant development time.

---

## License

By contributing to this project, you agree that your contributions will be licensed under the same license as the project.
